threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You spot someone in Imbibe asking for a flat white and look confused when they say it's not a café. Real locals know it's all about the takeout coffee.
Heard a group on the 59 arguing about whether Sainsbury's or Tesco has better meal deals. They didn’t notice the bus was moving until we hit a pothole.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw the old guy dancing with his parrot on a leash at Reuterplatz. Only in Neukölln, right?

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You know those random U-Bahn stations that feel more like art galleries? I saw a full-on yarn bombing at Bernaustrasse last week. Just another day in Berlin.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by how they navigate BART delays like it's a game. Tourists just stand there, puzzled, while we strategize our next move.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy on the 31 laughing as he rode past the Civic Center, said he missed the days when the bus was 'less of a circus.' Classic San Francisco.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists assume Gràcia's plazas are just for sipping café. Locals know they’re stages for spontaneous concerts and pop-up markets where the real life happens.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell when they're still using Google Maps to walk to places in Gràcia; locals just glide through the narrow streets without a second thought.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think it’s a good idea to get a taxi after midnight instead of the bus—trust me, the bus is a rite of passage here.
Heard a bloke on the 36 bus last night complaining about his mate's 'horrendous sock tan'. Only in Peckham, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a group of kids setting up a pop-up café in the park with no adults around, just their giggles and a makeshift sign. Pure Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, you can find spontaneous open-air jam sessions by the canal—no permits, just pure creativity spilling out into the night.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they instinctively know the best time to avoid BART delays and can name all the ghost kitchens in the area.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a kid try to catch a Muni bus by jogging alongside it, only to trip and fall. Bus driver just smirked and rolled on, like it was an everyday show.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: You'd think tourists would know tapas are a snack, not a meal. Watching them order a dozen and wonder why they can't move after is wild.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their bewildered look while trying to navigate the Santiago Rusiñol square – it’s a maze, not a shortcut.
